danger_signals.visual_form: Black fog covering unexplored map areas
danger_signals.meaning: Unknown territory; potential enemy or environmental threats beyond player vision

danger_signals.visual_form: Grey overlay gradually obscuring previously scouted areas
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of unit presence/vision in that area; enemy units may be moving there undetected

danger_signals.visual_form: Faction-colored units (Harkonnen red/black, Atreides blue, Ordos green/yellow variants)
Probes collecting samples from dead sandworms (campaign narrative visual)
danger_signals.meaning: Hidden agenda threat; danger to faction stability and control of Arrakis

safety_signals.visual_form: Base buildings with faction color schemes and architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Secure zone; spice harvesting and unit production location

safety_signals.visual_form: Fremen, Imperial Sardaukar, Guild, Ix, or Tleilaxu units with distinct visual markers
safety_signals.meaning: Support available; special unit access and strategic advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Mobile harvester units collecting spice in designated fields
safety_signals.meaning: Economic stability; resource flow securing base operations

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Atreides hawk sigil (inferred from Dune universe)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Blue-colored enemy units with sonic weapon effects and mech designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Base architecture reflecting noble/honorable aesthetic; mech and sonic weapon visual effects

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red/black-colored enemy units with flame weapon effects and tank-heavy designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Brutalist, industrial base architecture; flame weapon visual effects (fire, explosions)

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Green/yellow-colored enemy units with energy shield visual effects and agile designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sleek, technological base architecture; energy shield shimmer effects

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Allied units show distinct desert warrior aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Desert terrain integration; irregular formation patterns; campaign alliance with Atreides shown through unit cooperation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Probes and soldiers with flesh-harvest implied aesthetics; Emperor Worm as final threat visualization
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sinister biotechnological presence; water supply contamination (narrative); Emperor Worm as colossal environmental threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Black shroud covering entire map except base starting area
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must scout with units to reveal map and locate enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Portions of map revealed in full color; units visible with faction colors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy units respond to player vision; combat engagement possible

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Loss of unit presence in area
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Previously scouted area gradually covered by grey fog of war
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player loses real-time intelligence; enemy units may move through obscured areas undetected

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fremen units appear as allied faction; Fremen visual markers integrated with Atreides forces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fremen units join player missions; diplomatic narrative shifts to alliance-focused objectives

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dual baron leadership visual distinction; Gunseng vs Copec faction variance in unit appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Internal Harkonnen civil war objectives; player-chosen allegiance determines enemy faction identity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ghola Emperor visual markers; Imperial authority symbols integrated into Ordos faction display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ordos units gain legitimacy visual cues; propaganda messaging through unit behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Emperor Worm visualization as colossal worm creature; mind-control drug effects narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final campaign mission shows faction forces under mind-control; erratic ally behavior; desperate defense patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Victorious faction flag/sigil dominates screen; Arrakis and spice melange visually secured
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: FMV cutscene shows faction leader proclaimed Emperor; faction supremacy established

environmental_storytelling.example: Atreides bases feature noble, organized structure design; Harkonnen bases show brutal, industrial fortification; Ordos bases display sleek, technological aesthetics

environmental_storytelling.technique: Unit design language as faction identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Atreides sonic weapons and mechs visually communicate honor and advanced weaponry; Harkonnen flame tanks show brutality and chemical warfare; Ordos energy shields show technological sophistication
Atreides campaign map shows Fremen desert regions; Harkonnen shows Giedi Prime industrial homeworld; Ordos shows ghola creation facilities and puppet control centers

environmental_storytelling.technique: Ally/enemy unit visual distinction within same faction
environmental_storytelling.example: Captured enemy buildings produce faction-colored units; player can visually distinguish owned vs allied units through color overlay or formation markers
Early campaign shows probe activity collecting flesh; mid-campaign shows water supply contamination narrative; endgame shows colossal Emperor Worm as existential threat visualization

environmental_storytelling.technique: Full Motion Video cutscenes as faction characterization
environmental_storytelling.example: FMV scenes show Atreides nobles and diplomacy; Harkonnen show internal treachery and sibling rivalry; Ordos show manipulation and ghola control schemes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves to spice fields, collects resources, returns to refinery; presence indicates economic health
Moves in predictable patterns; becomes aggressive when player unit enters vision range
Returns fire; may retreat if damaged; faction-specific weapon effects visible (flame, sonic, energy)
Cooperates in missions; fights alongside Atreides units; shows loyalty through unit formation discipline
Probes collect samples; soldiers infiltrate and sabotage; behavior suggests hidden agenda vs open warfare
Erratic movement; may attack allies; shows impaired decision-making through illogical unit positioning

notes: Emperor: Battle for Dune uses classic RTS visual language (shroud, fog of war, faction colors) combined with Dune universe lore. Danger is communicated through map obscuration and enemy presence rather than environmental hazards. Political situation is conveyed through campaign narrative with FMV cutscenes and sub-faction alliances. World-state changes are reflected in available unit types (capturing enemy buildings grants faction unit access) and NPC faction behavior. The three main factions are visually distinct through color schemes, unit designs (sonic/mech vs flame/tank vs energy shield), and base architecture. Sub-factions like Fremen, Tleilaxu, and Imperial forces serve as story markers of campaign progression and allegiance.
